examples: To be sure, Grantham up until now hasn't been a die-hard inflationist.
The second scenario is just the opposite of the first one, but equally tragic: it consists of an "inflationist cure" so intense that a new bubble is created.
They can either allow the recessionary process to follow its course, and with it the healthy and painful readjustment, or they can escape forward toward a "renewed inflationist" cure.
All the while, these inflationist moves, while only showing spotty symptoms in the countries of origin, are lighting fires around the emerging markets.
This means Japanese Prime Minister Shinzo Abe will be able to appoint a new Governor that is more in line with his inflationist policies.
